patches.find: many orders of magnitude larger than Gaia's precision — and the dome cannot claim Gaia's measurements are distorted by the medium, because Gaia orbits at the Sun-Earth L2 point, outside the dome's cavity entirely
patches.replace: many orders of magnitude larger than Gaia's precision. Moreover, refraction is chromatic — different wavelengths are bent by different amounts. Gaia's optical astrometry agrees with VLBI radio astrometry to microarcsecond precision; if n(r) were real, optical and radio positions would diverge by arcseconds (see WIN-016). The dome cannot claim Gaia's measurements are distorted by the medium, because Gaia orbits at the Sun-Earth L2 point, outside the dome's cavity entirely
patches.rationale: ISS-842: Adds chromaticity bridge to Gaia argument — pre-empts the defender's 'systematic refraction preserves relative astrometry' counter by noting optical/radio agreement falsifies any large n(r). Cross-references WIN-016.

patches.find: the review structure, not the geometry, is what makes it interesting to evaluate.
patches.replace: This review evaluates every claimed prediction against independent data and the model's own internal consistency.
patches.rationale: ISS-846: Removes dismissive editorial judgment from SEC-1.1 TLDR that appeared before any evidence. The evaluation belongs in Part 9 (Conclusions) where it is earned by evidence, not as the first framing sentence readers see.
